property_issue_summary: The inspection identifies 7 impacted areas across the property.
The most consistent issue patterns are wet-area tile or plumbing defects, moisture ingress, dampness, or leakage, secondary spread into ceilings or adjacent areas.
The thermal report contains 30 observations, with 18 left unmatched where confidence was weak.

area_wise_observations.observation_summary: Hall Skirting level Dampness Common Bathroom tile hollowness Thermal evidence on 27/09/22 shows a hotspot of 28.8 C and a coldspot of 23.4 C.
area_wise_observations.probable_root_cause: Moisture ingress likely linked to wet-area tile joint failure, plumbing leakage, or poor sealing.
area_wise_observations.severity_reasoning: Severity is based on visible moisture-related distress is present, thermal spread reaches up to 5.4 C.
recommended_actions: Inspect bathroom and plumbing fixtures, then repair leaking joints, traps, or concealed lines.
Re-grout or reseal failed tile joints and replace hollow or loose tiles where needed.
Trace the moisture source before cosmetic repairs, then remove damaged plaster and repaint after drying.

additional_notes: Only evidence visible in the provided inspection and thermal documents has been included in this report.
Thermal observations that could not be tied to one area with enough confidence are shown separately instead of being forced into a conclusion.
18 thermal observations remain unresolved and should be reviewed manually.
